text:
The Kakin Military is the military of the Kakin Empire. They are also referred to as the Royal Army. Unconfirmed but unlikely to be untrue 

Overview

The Kakin Military is the military of the Kakin Empire. The Royal Army Office is on Tier 3 of the Black Whale 1, stationed in the political ward together with the political and legal enforcement agencies and the police station. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 

Enlistment

Kakin is known to have a Royal Military Academy that allows students to enroll from a young age. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 An enlistment age has not been given but the Royal Military Academy has a seventh grade, or classes for students 12-13 years old. It seemingly has not been around for very long, given that the second graduating class of students were expected to be placed in 2 nd Prince Camilla's personal army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 The structure of the school and its curriculum are unclear, though there has been mention of a field drill involving weapons with a speed record and an awards ceremony. The exams are said to not be difficult for those able to use Nen since birth. Though it has not been explicitly stated, it seems likely that graduates of the military academy go on to be part of Kakin's military; it is also unclear if prospective soldiers are able to be enlisted in Kakin's military without having gone through the Royal Military Academy. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 4 th Prince Tserriednich is known to have attended the Royal Military Academy. 

Kakin's Royal Military Academy is currently both the only known military academy of Kakin and the only known possible way by which people join the Kakin Military.

 
Known attendees of Kakin's Royal Military Academy (by chronological order of confirmation of attendance):
Tserriednich's Friend 1 
Tserriednich's Friend 2 
Tserriednich's Friend 3 
Momolly 
Gipper 
Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou 
Otocin 
Unnamed student shot in the leg by Tserriednich 
Longhi 
Makaha 
Borksen Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 
 

Structure

Though the full structure of Kakin's military is unknown, several ranks and positions have been mentioned in the story.
1 st Prince Benjamin is Deputy Military Adviser, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 though it is unclear if this is a rank in Kakin's military, a title, or some combination of them, and unclear how many Deputy Military Advisers Kakin has.
Balsamilco Might, the captain of Benjamin's guards, is a Master Sergeant. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 365 
Several characters in the Succession Contest arc have been revealed to be Corporals (abbreviated as Cpl.) in the Kakin Army, e.g. Mizuri, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 Maizan, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 Gipper. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395 
Borksen is a Private First Class (abbreviated as PFC). 
Otocin is a Private Second Class (abbreviated as PV2). 

The Kakin Military also has military doctors. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Attire and Appearance

Soldiers of the Kakin army all seemingly wear the same outfit, and their headgear seemingly seemingly serves to identify their rank. They don fair-colored combat gear, comprising of a jacket, pants, and boots. Kakin soldiers have also been seen armed with assault rifles. Soldiers' headgear seemingly serves to identify their rank, Corporals have only been seen wearing black round helmets, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 379 and PV2s have only been seen wearing berets. Several soldiers have been seen wearing patrol caps, though the ranks of soldiers wearing them are unclear, and several Kakin government employees not dressed in military wear but performing duties the military are responsible for have been seen wearing patrol caps with a Kakin insignia on it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

Roles and Duties

During Kakin's voyage to the pretend "Dark Continent", the Black Whale operates under martial law. The Royal Army acts as a neutral party in the Succession Battle and is assigned various tasks on board. The through-ways between the ship's tiers are guarded by the Royal Army. The military also manages all phone lines present in the ship, as well as monitoring the halls and keeping track of all activities. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 The Royal Army was shown to handle the deceased bodies of bodyguards and princes alike by carrying them outside a prince's quarters. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 They also have the authority to restrain suspects and take them into custody for further questioning. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 361 

Botobai and Mizaistom assist the military by taking command of private security. Both negotiated to obtain the passenger list of the Black Whale No.1 by getting the V6 to contact Kakin on their behalf. Botobai believed they would be able to reach an agreement thanks to the strong channels Begerossé Union and United States of Saherta have with Kakin. Mizaistom was also responsible for performing background checks of candidates. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 346 He is currently in the Royal Army conference room, while Botobai is located in the central courthouse of the Black Whale. Saccho plans to talk to Mizaistom and suggests a rotation so either him, Kanzai or Saiyu go support the Royal Army instead of "babysitting" Beyond. 

Privates in the Kakin Military have not been seen operating alone, always in groups, which are sometimes but not always led by a higher-ranking officer. Corporals have been seen managing incidents by themselves and leading small groups. 

The Kakin Military is known to have developed numerous weapons of mass destruction, including TSK-17 (though this was considered a failure when flaws were found during development). Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 A "military sign language" exists, and has been seen used when verbal communication is impractical. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 All soldiers have a unique service number. 

The Kakin Military's jurisdiction does not extend to the Justice Bureau, the Justice Bureau is seen with its own set of armed soldiers (or guards) in a distinctly different uniform. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

During Special Martial Law, soldiers of the royal army are granted the right to shoot anyone suspicious without warning. They have the duty to remove any armed citizens and eliminate subversives. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 409 

Personal Armies

Several Princes are known to have personal armies (also called private armies); Princes Benjamin, Camilla, Zhang Lei, and Tserriednich are pictured when Oito mentions that several Princes have personal armies, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 though no mentions of a personal army for Zhang Lei exist. The full strength of each Prince's personal armies have not been seen. Notably, Tserriednich's personal army seems to involve soldiers currently members of the royal army, Camilla's personal army was to be comprised of graduates from the Royal Military Academy until she intervened, and Benjamin's characterization makes it difficult to imagine a personal army that did not involve members of the Royal Army.

Society

Despite the Black Whale 1 operating under martial law, it is unclear if royal privilege supersedes the authority of the military. In a conversation with Benjamin about his missing man Musse, Kaiser (a Justice Bureau official) suggests that he could invoke his faction's authority and conduct a search of all Princes' quarters for Musse, which Benjamin suggests is pointless due to the ongoing court case about whether royal privilege or the military's authority has priority. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 Given that the language used is impersonal, it currently remains ambiguous as to whether Kaiser intended to invoke the military's authority, in which case nothing can be said about the Justice Bureau's privilege relative to the military, or whether he intended to invoke the Justice Bureau's authority, which when considering Benjamin's response suggests that it is inferior to both the military's authority and the royal privilege.

The Royal Military Academy functions in at least a small part to serve the Hui Guo Rou royal family. The second class of graduates was intended to become the personal army of Prince Camilla, though this did not end up being the case. 

Certain soldiers are aware of the importance of the Kakin Empire's three Mafia Families in maintaining the balance of society. At times, they have been seen not carrying out proper procedures in order to get the mafia's assistance to smoothly handle issues, like not immediately reporting their discovery of Lynch's body during a sweep for security and instead waiting to hand it over to the Xi-Yu Family. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 

At some point in time before the story, Beyond set up sham marriages involving Kakin military officers, who raised Beyond's children as their own and placed them into the Royal Military Academy. The number of children estimated to be in this situation is likely around 10-15. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

While Kakin's royal family and the country celebrate during the eve festival of the Black Whale 1's voyage, Balsamilco briefs his men about how the ship would operate during the voyage, mentioning that Kakin's Royal Army would be guarding the throughways between tiers, and that the ship would operate under martial law. Simultaneously elsewhere, Kurapika is informed by his employer 8 th Queen Oito Hui Guo Rou that the military would manage the phone lines. Oito also lets Kurapika know that the Royal Army would be a neutral party to the succession battle, and would monitor the halls and keep track of all activities. Shortly after, Kurapika gets a call from Mizaistom, informing Kurapika that he and Botobai are assisting the military to take command of private security. Kurapika informs Oito that in case of an emergency allowing the gate between Tiers 2 and 3 to be opened, they might be able to sneak her and her daughter 14 th Prince Woble past the other Princes and the army and blend in with the public to hide. 

The situation in the lower tiers becomes more chaotic on the first day of the voyage as the Black Whale departs. Medical assistants on Tier 3 with Cheadle and Leorio decide to request for soldiers at the pharmacy. At Tier 3's Central Courthouse, soldiers stand guard as Botobai judges passengers standing trial for causing trouble. In the Royal Army's conference room on Tier 4, Mizaistom conducts a briefing for many attendees. In Room 1014 on Tier 1, Kurapika and several of Woble's other guards discover the exsanguinated corpse of fellow guard Woody in their bathroom, which is taken away by the army. The discussion surrounding Woody's death reveals that several guards believe that it could be part of an internal conflict within the Kakin army. 

After escorting Oito and Woble back to Room 1014 from the departure ceremony on Tier 1, Nen beasts swarm Room 1014 and a guard is manipulated and kills two more guards. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 Kurapika restrains him and he is taken away by the army for questioning together with the new corpses. 

Shortly after 1 st Prince Benjamin departs from the departure ceremony, he orders his men, all soldiers of the royal army, to join the guards of the other Princes and spy on them, with permission to use lethal force in the name of self-defense. Vincent is assigned to Room 1014 and immediately kills the servant who receives him in "self-defense". He is restrained by Kurapika and takes a suicide pill to prevent being forced to testify against his employer. Kurapika instructs Bill to contact the military and hand him over. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 364 

In Room 1007, while smoking a legal drug with his guard Basho, 7 th Prince Luzurus muses about the possibility of using the drug to rehabilitate drug addicts. He notes that getting through Benjamin would be a problem, since drug control is the military's job. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

Later that day, Room 1014 learns of 12 th Prince Momoze being strangled in her bed in Room 1012. Babimyna, Vincent's replacement, helps them contact Room 1012, but they are unable to respond quickly enough, and Momoze dies. The royal army comes to carry her body out and conduct investigations on the room. Her mother, 7 th Queen Sevanti, reports Momoze's six guards to the King, and they are taken into custody to face a court martial. 

Kurapika begins conducting Nen lessons the next day in Room 1014, for up to two representatives from each Prince. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 One of them dies to a mysterious Nen attack Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 and another is arrested by the royal army for causing the distraction that led to the death. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

From Room 1013, Hanzo finishes his shift and decides to activate his doppelganger to avenge Momoze, his former employer. He pilots his doppelganger to the holding cells where her six former guards are held and manages to get the culprit to confess the truth by pretending that a deal with the army had been made and that Benjamin wanted to headhunt him. He returns to Room 1013 only to find its contents and occupants missing, and so heads to Room 1012 to try and use its phone to contact Room 1013. He finds that Room 1012 has been cordoned off by the army, and one of the soldiers stationed outside to guard the room informs Hanzo that the quarters of Princes who drop out are sealed and that the phone lines are cut. Hanzo heads to Room 1014 to get Kurapika's assistance in understanding the situation, mentioning that if the phone in Room 1013 were only broken and not blocked by Nen, the army should have fixed it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 

On the third day of the voyage, Halkenburg learns of Momoze's death and makes his way to Nasubi's quarters to try and prevent more deaths, despite his men pointing out that he would not be able to. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 He meets two soldiers standing guard at the entrance to Nasubi's quarters and is refused entry, but convinces the interlocutor to accept a letter for Nasubi instead and promises to keep returning each day until he is allowed entry. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

Elsewhere, a court trial between Benjamin and 2 nd Prince Camilla begins because of an attack by Camilla on Benjamin the previous day, resulting in Benjamin's man Musse vanishing after being caught up in Camilla's ability. Both sides demand that the other hand over Musse, and the presiding judge Cleapatro confines both Princes to the VVIP area under surveillance until Musse is found, later requesting for the royal army to search both quarters. 

At 10:05 a.m. that day, another participant of Kurapika's Nen lessons dies in Room 1014's bathroom. One participant calls for the royal army to be informed, accusing Kurapika of luring them to Room 1014 with the promise of teaching them Nen before eliminating them one by one. While Kurapika worries about how to persuade them to continue and prevent the royal army from being involved, Belerainte, a Hunter participant, defuses the situation by pointing out how careless the latest victim had been. Belerainte points out that the royal army should only be called to recover bodies, and that he could accuse all the participants of the same thing they were accusing Kurapika of. 

On Tier 5, the Cha-R Mafia Family's territory, a group of Cha-R members see the Phantom Troupe members gathered and offer to help them with their goals if they disperse. Chrollo tells the Cha-R group's leader of their goal of finding Hisoka and gives him a rough description of Hisoka. The Cha-R group's leader offers to check the royal army's passenger list, but tells Chrollo that unofficial passengers are also on board. He also permits Chrollo to search Tier 5, but lets him know that the Troupe would need a ticket to get into Tiers 3 and 4, and that the royal army manages the ticket punchers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 

The Cha-R and Xi-Yu Family bosses are informed of the Phantom Troupe's presence and goals, and both instruct their respective underbosses to find Hisoka before the Troupe does. Hinrigh, the Xi-Yu underboss, gives his underlings instructions to ask for 3 rd Queen Tang Zhao Li Tang Zhao Li is not explicitly identified by name but the Queen in reference can be surmised to be her due to the fact that her only son is the benefactor of the mafia family that Hinrigh is a part of. to get the soldiers to do a round of I.D. and ticket verification. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 

In Tier 3's general passenger area, two soldiers cordon off a hallway while more investigate the area. Mizaistom approaches the scene and is briefed of someone, disguised as a royal army soldier, who killed twenty people quickly without any signs of Nen being used. As he walks through the hallway, he passes by many soldiers managing the scene. Mizaistom speaks to the the witness, unaware that she and the assailant were fellow Heil-Ly members that had coordinated a testimony to test Mizaistom with. She recounts how she hid until the royal army pried open the door and found the bathroom empty. Mizaistom points out that even a rat could not fit through the pipes, and Cashew insists that although the soldiers had said the same thing, he did not come out and the soldiers had confirmed that the door was locked. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 379 

Later that day, Mizaistom briefs a group of army officials on the crimes on board and suggests that the criminals were multiple solo serial killers acting in parallel, motivated by the ritualistic seed urn ceremony to decide the next king. The officials react with hostility at the implication that a Prince was behind the crimes, but Mizaistom points out that if a Prince were indeed behind the situation, a scapegoat could easily resolve the situation. Mizaistom lets the officials know his expectation that the crimes will continue and multiply, and eventually sink the ship once a critical point had been reached. After pausing to let his point sink in, he points out that the lower tiers have a ratio of over 300 civilians per soldier, making them powerless in an actual situation, and proposes moving 800 of the upper tiers' combined 1400 soldiers to the lower tiers. He is met with another wave of resistance from the officials, but points out that the fate of Kakin itself is on the line, and expresses his confidence that Benjamin would agree with their advice. As the meeting wraps up, he adds that the officials would be in trouble if they did not advise Benjamin, since their discussion points remained in the meeting minutes. 

At 10:00 a.m. that day, an announcement is made across Tier 3 of a stowaway with a deadly weapon being on the loose. Passengers are instructed to remain in their cabins and to keep their doors closed until instructed otherwise, and informed that they would be escorted by the military to their meals, with mealtimes allocated by area. Soldiers direct crowds of passengers back to their cabins. Botobai is seen leading soldiers in a sweep beginning on Tier 3, instructing them to detain anyone without an I.D. ticket, even children. Soldiers interview passengers at gunpoint in their cabins, turning out their sleeping bunks. 

As soldiers sweep the open areas of Tier 3, they make their way to the Observation Deck. They see two people there and instruct them to raise their hands and turn around slowly. Illumi and Kalluto Zoldyck comply, and say that they had not heard the announcement because they were asleep. With his consent, a corporal reaches into Illumi's pocket to check his I.D. ticket, and after reading it, respectfully asks what a VVIP like him was doing there. Illumi vaguely volunteers that they had business, and the corporal politely informs him that due to the recently imposed martial law, the passage between Tier 2 and 3 leading to the upper tiers would be closed, probably for the duration of the voyage. He offers to escort them up, but Illumi states his preference for remaining there. Botobai informs the corporal that Illumi is also a Hunter, and the corporal defers handling the situation to him and Mizaistom. 

While Mizaistom tries to figure out how best to act with the information he gained from speaking to Illumi, a subordinate of his runs over to inform him of a special person found without an I.D. Mizaistom immediately heads back to his headquarters to deal with the situation. While on the way, he checks if the special person is safe, and his subordinate informs him that several soldiers had recognized her. Mizaistom orders gag orders imposed on them and for them to be placed into custody, then, at his headquarters, speaks with 11 th Prince Fugetsu. While he tries to interview Fugetsu, a subordinate of his informs him that the surveillance footage from the passageways obtained from the royal army did not show Fugetsu using the existing corridors. Fugetsu is eventually escorted back to Room 1011 by Mizaistom. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 

In Room 1008, Rihan eliminates 8 th Prince Salé-salé's Guardian Spirit Beast and reports his success through the private channel of Benjamin's soldiers. Yushohi, assigned to guard Fugetsu, speaks next and reports that the royal army's report strongly suggests that Fugetsu's Nen Beast is likely capable of translocation. At 8:00 p.m. the next day, an announcement is made notifying the public that the stowaway had been arrested and that the lockdown order had been lifted. 

On the sixth day of the voyage soldiers standing guard at the VVIP area smile at Halkenburg as he makes his fourth consecutive daily visit, expecting him to repeat his previous days' actions. The guard managing the gate to the VVIP area greets him and asks if he had another letter for Nasubi. Suddenly, Halkenburg and his men draw arms and point them at the soldiers there. The soldiers, unprepared for the situation, are forced to let him in. After a short while, a trio of soldiers accompany Nugui in, where Nugui explains to Halkenburg that his situation had already been accounted for as a secret training exercise. Halkenburg concedes and withdraws back to Room 1009 with a newfound resolve. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

During the Sunday banquet in the evening of the eighth day of voyage, 10 th Prince Kacho and Fugetsu attempt to escape. After Melody leaves the waiting area to perform, a soldier standing guard perks up when he notices Kacho and Fugetsu put in earpieces, but returns to his duties after hearing them explain that they were going over the music for their performance. Melody's music transfixes all listeners, including soldiers in the tier who hear her music over Tier 1's speaker system. The twin Princes rush through the hallways past collapsed soldiers, through the entrance area past hypnotized soldiers, and make it through more unresponsive soldiers standing guard at the emergency area where the lifeboats are kept. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 

On the seventh day of the voyage, Cha-R higherups Ken'i Wang and Tajao discuss the situation with the Heil-Ly, mentioning that the army will soon suspect that the mafia is involved. Ken'i suggests a bust to find out what the Heil-Ly is up to, but adds that their hideout would also be exposed to scrutiny. Shortly after in Room 1004, Tserriednich sends orders for his soldiers on the lower tiers to search Heil-Ly boss Morena Prudo's hideout. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 

On the ninth day of the voyage, soldiers are seen outside the Justice Bureau on Tier 2. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the tenth day of the voyage, Balsamilco has Halkenburg arrested under suspicion of premeditated murder of Benjamin's men. Soldiers escort a small delegation from the Justice Bureau to Room 1009 to effect the arrest. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 Later that day, soldiers are seen standing guard along the hallways as Zhang Lei makes his way through Tier 1, from his residence at Room 1003 to Xi-Yu boss Onior Longbao's residence in the VVIP area. After Zhang Lei leaves, Onior contacts Hinrigh, and gives him instructions to head to Tier 3 to find Hisoka and kill Morena. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 390 

Hinrigh heads up from Tier 4 to Tier 3 with Zakuro and Lynch, two of his underlings, and bribes soldiers to let them upstairs. During the exchange, the soldier lets them know that although Tier 3 had calmed down, the Heil-Ly were still on a rampage, and instructs them to do something about it soon. Hinrigh ignores the slight and as the group head upwards, he mentioning to his underlings that the soldiers' motivations are proportional to the tier they were on. On Tier 3, they encounter some Heil-Ly members, who split up at the sight of them. Hinrigh tells his underlings to deal with the ones remaining and tails the one who left, following her into a hallway as she walks into an area restricted from mafia. At its entrance, soldiers stop him from following the Heil-Ly member, who then taunts him by saying that the soldiers would soon stop turning a blind eye to the others and arrest them. He realizes that the Heil-Ly did not include their members on the mafia member list, and tells the Heil-Ly member to have Morena challenge them. 

Hinrigh returns, escorted by soldiers to his underlings to break up their fight and stop them from continuing to beat up their already defeated opponents. He lets them know that their "mafia" opponents were officially civilians, and that the situation would get complicated even though the military could be flexible. The soldier tells them that they used excessive force, but seeing as it was self defense, he would let them go as long as they stayed out of Tier 3, and warns them that they would be arrested if they were seen again. Hinrigh apologizes to the soldiers and bribes them with money, tapping their guns with rolls of cash and telling them that they would not need to be used. As they leave, the soldiers laugh at how they benefited from all 3 gangs just by standing there, but their guns turn into snakes and kill them. 

The crowd starts panicking and calls for other soldiers are heard. Hinrigh splits up from Zakuro and Lynch, and in another area, enlists a nearby soldier's help to check the passengers' cameras for signs of the Heil-Ly. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 391 Shortly after, soldiers are called to the scene of another commotion where gunshots had been heard to address the situation, and the army finds Hinrigh again. The corporal that comes to manage the situation answers some of the crowd's questions, including one about the allegations of missing or dead passengers in the lower tiers, by saying that the serial killer was arrested, and that it was a matter between mafia families. The crowd are surprised when they notice the person fighting with Hinrigh getting to his feet, having believed that he had died. Two soldiers follow the corporal as he hears from the "Misha Hao#victim" that he did not want to press charges, then turns his attention to Hinrigh and confirms his identity. He lets the "civilian" go, but turns to Hinrigh, and quietly offers to sell him information on the location of the Heil-Ly's boss. After some negotiation, they come to an agreement, and the corporal agrees to be part of the police raid on their location, so that he can also escort Hinrigh there. After they come to an agreement, the corporal disperses the crowd. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 392 

Elsewhere on Tier 3, Tsudonke, a Cha-R underling, speaks with a shopowner on Tier 3 to try and learn more about the recent conflict. He asks if the shop owner was able to ask anyone in the military, but his question is ignored, and instead, the shop owner tells him that the soldiers on the lower tiers could be bribed for information. Tsudonke returns back to Ken'i and the Cha-R group on Tier 3, and Ken'i has half the group return to their hideout while the rest continue to search Tier 3. 

While the corporal waits for Hinrigh to return with the money, Ken'i comes across him and asks to be cut into the deal. The corporal agrees, and eventually leads both underbosses to Room 3101, located in Tier 3's first class cabins. Hinrigh encourages him to initiate the raid, suggesting that whoever is inside would probably not attack military, but tells him to turn his gun's safety off just in case. The corporal uses his authority as part of the Kakin army to get the room's occupant to open the door, but vanishes as soon as he steps into the room. Hinrigh and Ken'i peer into the room and find an old man sitting on the bed with his hands raised, being unnaturally chatty and pointing out that the two underbosses did not look like military. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 They identify the room as a trap which teleports people who enter the room, and decide to leave investigating the trap to the Spiders. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 

In the Heil-Ly hideout, Morena briefs some of her underlings and tells her that they were being noticed, and that they were only going to find military and mafia knocking at their supposedly inconspicuous door from then on. One of them mentions that if Benjamin's men came, they had no lawful basis to send them away. 

A group of soldiers, part of Tserriednich's private army, force their way into the Heil-Ly's official hideout and discover the corpses of two Cha-R underlings there. They discuss how best to sweep Tier 3 to find Morena without drawing the attention of the Royal Army or the Police. One of them asks if they should discuss how to act in the case that the Heil-Ly had Nen users, and they resume patrolling the area. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 395 

As they patrol, they discuss their findings, and one of them points out that military normally used their discretion when handling mafia scuffles without civilians involved. They speculate about the possibility that the government might try to eliminate the mafia, since getting rid of them was convenient for many groups including the military. Later, they suggest making transfer requests within a reasonable frequency, since any purge would be kept secret from soldiers like them; throughways would be closed then with access limited to the royal army, and so their transfer requests being rejected acts as a sign that a purge is being carried out. Their commander points out that they were smart and could have been in the royal army by then, but hears that they find it too much of a hassle to do so. 

As Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan investigate the mysterious room for Ken'i, he approaches them and introduces Hinrigh to them, who proposes stepping into the teleportation door and dropping a transmitter there. He and Nobunaga step through the trap Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 398 and manage to hide the transmitter before being forced out, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 and they split up after. Nobunaga reunites with Phinks and Feitan, and together they head down to Tier 4 to see if the transmitter was above or below them. Soldiers stationed at the throughways let them pass, having been informed by the mafia to let them through. 

Outside Room 1010, soldiers and bodyguards face each other, the soldiers having come to cordon off the room, and the bodyguards there at Queen Seiko's orders to prevent the room from being cordoned off. 

On the eleventh day of the voyage, a Justice Bureau escort heading to the VVIP area passes by soldiers guarding the hallways of Tier 1. More soldier are seen standing in the cordoned-off hallway of Room 1001. Later that day, Halkenburg collapses, and soldiers are seen in the Central Hospital on Tier 3 after Benjamin sends the royal and military medical teams over. 

Halkenburg dies the next day, and preparations begin for a funeral procession beginning on Tier 3 and moving upwards to Tier 1. Lynch's corpse is discovered by some soldiers doing a sweep, and the Xi-Yu Family are contacted to handle the body. The soldiers agree not to bother with a report of the matter. When the funeral procession begins, soldiers stand along a cordoned path at the entrance of the Central Hospital to watch the crowd. When Halkenburg's casket, guarded by a military escort, makes its way along the path, soldiers face inward. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 Soldiers stand guard at the ends of the large staircase between Tiers 2 and 3. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Special Martial Law is declared, Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 granting soldiers the right to shoot without warning, and making it their duty to remove armed citizens and eliminate subversives. Orders are given for passengers to listen to the orders of the military police and the royal army Soldiers are seen processing and clearing citizens of suspicion on Tier 3. A private reports a 'Type S' incident (a supernatural incident) to his corporal, where soldiers who stepped into the entrance of Room 3101 vanished. After investigating the scene, the corporal gives the orders to report the matter to Tier 1. Benjamin, leading the operation, hears of the matter, and delegates a squad to handle the matter as he focuses his attention to other matters. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

Benjamin makes his way to the Justice Department on Tier 2, where he declares his intention to consolidate the three branches of government by military force. He informs his witnesses of a suspected bioterrorism incident, resulting in his decision to forcibly isolate the Princes and have soldiers under his command watch them. 

Trivia

In Chapter 361, the cap of a Kakin Army's soldier seemingly has the word "Kakin" in a stylized way on it, confirming the in-universe name of the nation. 

References
